Here in Kings Heath

A local mortgage broker that feels more like a friendly neighbour.

Clear, independent mortgage advice for people buying, moving and remortgaging around Kings Heath and Birmingham. Warm conversations, no jargon, and support right through to the keys.

Book A Chat See how it works
Local. Independent. Lovely.

Calm advice. Clear next steps.

And a real person at the end of the phone.

Independent adviceOptions chosen around you—not one bank.
Evening appointmentsMortgage chats that fit around real life.
No awkward sales pitchUseful guidance, with absolutely no pressure.
Quick mortgage calculator

A useful starting point, in under a minute.

Illustration based on a 4.75% repayment mortgage.

A different sort of broker

Big financial decisions.Very human

Mortgages can be complicated. Speaking to your broker should not be. We take time to understand the life around the numbers, then explain your options like a neighbour would—clearly, patiently and without showing off.

🏡Friendly advice from just down the road.

What we help with

Whatever home looks like next.

Buying, moving, remortgaging or protecting the people you love — we'll help make the mortgage side feel clear, calm and manageable.

How we work

Three simple steps. No disappearing acts. No confusing jargon.

You will always know where things stand, what we need from you and what happens next.

Let's start with a chat
01

A proper chat

Tell us what you are hoping to do. We will listen, ask useful questions and make the next step feel clearer.

02

We do the digging

We search the market, make sense of the small print and talk you through the options worth considering.

03

Right through to keys

From application to completion, we keep an eye on the details and keep you in the loop.

The Eaton approach

Mortgage advice with a good bedside manner.

Meet Deb

The numbers matter. But so does how you feel getting there.

Eaton Mortgages was built around a simple idea: people deserve expert advice without being made to feel out of their depth.

Deb brings calm thinking, honest explanations and a very human approach to every conversation—whether you are buying for the first time or have moved more times than you care to count.

Based in Kings Heath

Helping people across Birmingham and beyond

Recently helped

Real moves. Real people. Just down the road.

Every mortgage has a life happening around it. Here are a few of the situations we help make simpler.

Kind words

From people who have been in your shoes.

The nicest proof is hearing that someone felt informed, supported and a little less stressed.

“Everything was explained in plain English and we felt looked after from the first call. It never felt like we were asking a silly question.”

“Fast replies, calm advice and a genuinely friendly approach. We always knew what was happening and what came next.”

Four seasons before lunch?

Peggy Gou

Good questions

Things people often ask before we chat.

And if yours is not here, ask away. There are no daft mortgage questions.

How much does mortgage advice cost?
We will explain any fee clearly before you decide to go ahead. The first conversation is simply a chance to understand what you need and whether we are the right fit.
Can we speak in the evening?
Yes. Mortgages have an awkward habit of arriving during busy lives, so evening appointments are available when daytime calls are difficult.
Can you help if I am self-employed?
Absolutely. We can look at lenders who understand company directors, sole traders, contractors and income that varies from month to month.
Do I need to have found a property first?
Not at all. In fact, speaking before you start viewing can give you a much clearer sense of budget and help you make an offer with confidence.
What should I bring to our first chat?
Nothing elaborate. A rough idea of your income, savings, existing commitments and what you hope to do is enough for an initial conversation.
No pressure. Just a useful conversation.

Ready when you are. Kettle's on.

Tell us what you are planning and we will find a good time to talk it through. Video call, phone call or a local coffee—whatever feels easiest.

A relaxed phone call

Usually 20–30 minutes

A video appointment

From your sofa, naturally

An in-person chat

Meet locally over a coffee

A proper email

If typing it out is easier
